sizing_rule.note: Dynamic fractional f applied to active equity portion. Lower initial active allocation (e.g., 0.1 vs 0.2) accelerates convergence to optimal f. Max drawdown tolerance example: 25% equity retracement. Uses account segmentation (active/inactive equity) with dynamic optimization.

source_rule_text: Divide account equity into active and inactive portions, with dynamic fractional f overtaking static fractional f faster when initial active equity allocation is lower (e.g., 0.1 vs 0.2).
